rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding or modifying AGREE annex clauses in the current component's scope
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type scopes
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions; use exposed ports or constants instead
generic_rules: Minimize repairs by focusing on the diagnostic context and visible identifiers
Avoid changing unrelated architecture; only modify the current component's annex
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Ensure helper declarations align with the actual type of the expression
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` for helper declarations and `const` for constants
Boolean expressions should evaluate to bool type
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not visible in the model
Do not add architecture to fix AGREE errors; remove invalid clauses instead
irrelevant_topics: AADL timing properties and unit tokens in AGREE expressions
AGREE assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports in implementation scopes
